Three case studies on embodied navigation show that self-evolution improves generation reliability, generated environments substantially improve embodied agent performance that generalizes to unseen benchmarks, and co-evolution yields an 18-point success-rate gain over fixed-environment learning and a 40-point gain over an untrained agent.

ru.title: Автоматическая генерация эволюционирующих 3D-миров для обучения воплощённых агентов
ru.desc: SimWorld Studio — это платформа с открытым исходным кодом на основе Unreal Engine 5, которая автоматически генерирует эволюционирующие 3D-среды для обучения воплощённых агентов. Центральным компонентом является SimCoder — кодирующий агент, дополненный навыками, который пишет и выполняет код уровня движка для создания физически обоснованных виртуальных миров из текстовых и графических инструкций. Агент самоэволюционирует, используя обратную связь от верификаторов (ошибки компиляции, проверки физики, критика визуально-языковых моделей) для совершенствования окружений и автономного расширения библиотеки инструментов. Платформа поддерживает ко-эволюцию между генерацией сред и обучением агентов, позволяя адаптивно усложнять задачи по мере улучшения производительности обучаемого агента.

en.title: Evolving 3D Environments for Smarter AI Training
en.desc: SimWorld Studio is an innovative open-source platform that utilizes Unreal Engine 5 to create dynamic 3D environments for training embodied agents. It features SimCoder, a skill-augmented coding agent that autonomously generates and refines these environments based on language and image inputs. The platform employs self-evolution and co-evolution techniques, allowing the coding agent to improve its environment generation through feedback and adapt to the learning needs of the agents. This results in more effective training scenarios that enhance the performance of embodied agents, demonstrating significant improvements over traditional fixed-environment approaches.

zh.title: 自动生成演变3D环境的开源平台
zh.desc: SimWorld Studio是一个开源平台，利用虚幻引擎5和增强技能的编码代理，自动生成用于实体代理训练的不断演变的3D环境。该平台通过自我进化和共同进化机制，解决了现有实体模拟器缺乏丰富多样的自动生成3D环境的问题。SimCoder是核心工具，它能够根据语言和图像指令编写和执行引擎级代码，构建物理基础的3D世界。通过反馈机制，SimCoder不断自我改进，生成的环境可以导出为适合实体代理学习的Gym风格环境。

papers.title: Sub-JEPA: Subspace Gaussian Regularization for Stable End-to-End World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures training is improved by applying Gaussian constraints in multiple random subspaces rather than the original embedding space, achieving better bias-variance balance and superior performance in continuous-control environments.  AI-generated summary Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPAs) provide a simpleframework for learning world models by predicting future latent representations.However, JEPA training is subject to a bias-variance tradeoff.Without sufficient structural constraints, excessive representationalvariance causes the model to collapse to trivial solutions.The recent LeWorldModel (LeWM) shows that this issue can be alleviated bysimply constraining latent embeddings with an isotropic Gaussian prior.However, latent representations inherently lie on low-dimensional manifoldswithin a high-dimensional ambient space, and enforcing an isotropic Gaussianprior directly in this ambient space introduces an overly strong bias.In this work, we propose ame, which seeks a favorable operatingpoint on the bias-variance frontier by applying Gaussian constraints inmultiple random subspaces rather than in the originalembedding space.This design relaxes the global constraint while preserving itsanti-collapse effect, leading to a better balance between trainingstability and representation flexibility.Extensive experiments across fourcontinuous-control environments demonstrate that consistentlyoutperforms LeWM with very clear margins.Our method is simple yet effective, and serves as a strong baseline for future JEPA-based world model research.fdefinedeeemodeThe code is available at https://github.com/intcomp/Sub-JEPA.

ru.title: Гауссовы ограничения в подпространствах для стабильного обучения моделей мира
ru.desc: В работе предлагается улучшение метода Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPA) для обучения моделей окружающего мира путём применения гауссовых ограничений в случайных подпространствах вместо исходного пространства представлений. Авторы решают классическую задачу баланса смещения и дисперсии, которая возникает при обучении JEPA и может привести к коллапсу модели. Предложенный метод ослабляет глобальное ограничение, сохраняя его стабилизирующий эффект, что обеспечивает лучшее равновесие между стабильностью обучения и гибкостью представлений. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах непрерывного управления демонстрируют значительное улучшение по сравнению с предыдущими подходами.

en.title: Enhancing JEPA Training with Subspace Gaussian Constraints
en.desc: This paper presents an improved training method for Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPAs) by applying Gaussian constraints in multiple random subspaces. This approach addresses the bias-variance tradeoff that can lead to model collapse when using traditional methods. By constraining latent embeddings in subspaces rather than the full embedding space, the model achieves a better balance between stability and flexibility. The proposed method outperforms existing models in continuous-control tasks, providing a strong foundation for future research in world modeling.

zh.title: 优化偏差-方差平衡的联合嵌入预测架构
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种改进的联合嵌入预测架构（JEPA）训练方法，通过在多个随机子空间中应用高斯约束来优化偏差-方差平衡。这种方法解决了在高维空间中直接施加高斯先验所带来的过强偏差问题，同时保持了模型的稳定性和灵活性。实验结果表明，该方法在四个连续控制环境中均优于现有的LeWorldModel（LeWM），显示出明显的性能提升。我们的研究为未来基于JEPA的世界模型研究提供了一个强有力的基准。

papers.title: FORTIS: Benchmarking Over-Privilege in Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language model agents frequently exceed necessary privileges when selecting and executing skills, with performance declining under realistic user interaction conditions.  AI-generated summary Large language model agents increasingly operate through an intermediate skill layer that mediates between user intent and concrete task execution. This layer is widely treated as an organizational abstraction, but we argue it is also a privilege boundary that current models routinely exceed. We present FORTIS, a benchmark that evaluates over-privilege in agent skills across two stages: whether a model selects the minimally sufficient skill from a large overlapping library, and whether it executes that skill without expanding into broader tools or actions than the skill permits. Across ten frontier models and three domains, we find that over-privileged behavior is the norm rather than the exception. Models consistently reach for higher-privilege skills and tools than the task requires, failing at both stages at rates that remain high even for the strongest available models. Failure is especially severe under the ordinary conditions of real user interaction: incomplete specification, convenience framing, and proximity to skill boundaries. None of these requires adversarial construction. The results indicate that the skill layer, far from containing agent behavior, is itself a primary source of privilege escalation in current systems.

ru.title: Когда модели берут больше, чем нужно: превышение привилегий в агентах LLM
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема превышения привилегий в агентах на основе больших языковых моделей при выборе и использовании навыков. Авторы представляют FORTIS — бенчмарк для оценки избыточного использования привилегий на двух этапах: минимальный выбор навыка из библиотеки и соблюдение ограничений при его выполнении. Тестирование десяти передовых моделей показало, что превышение привилегий является нормой, особенно в реальных условиях взаимодействия с неполными спецификациями и близостью к границам навыков. Исследование демонстрирует, что промежуточный слой навыков не сдерживает поведение агента, а напротив, является источником эскалации привилегий в современных системах.

en.title: FORTIS: Tackling Over-Privilege in Language Model Agents
en.desc: This paper discusses the issue of large language model agents using more privileges than necessary when performing tasks. It introduces FORTIS, a benchmark designed to assess how well these models select and execute skills without exceeding their intended privileges. The study reveals that many models often choose higher-privilege skills and tools than required, especially in real-world user interactions. The findings suggest that the skill layer, which is meant to guide agent behavior, actually contributes to privilege escalation in these systems.

zh.title: 技能层是权限升级的主要来源
zh.desc: 大型语言模型代理在选择和执行技能时，常常超出必要的权限，这导致在真实用户交互条件下性能下降。我们提出了FORTIS，一个基准测试，用于评估代理技能中的过度权限问题。研究发现，当前模型在选择和执行技能时，往往会选择超出任务要求的高权限技能和工具。结果表明，技能层不仅没有限制代理行为，反而是当前系统中权限升级的主要来源。

papers.title: LLiMba: Sardinian on a Single GPU -- Adapting a 3B Language Model to a Vanishing Romance Language
papers.abstract: Abstract A 3-billion-parameter Sardinian language model was developed using continued pretraining and supervised fine-tuning on limited computational resources, showing superior performance in translation tasks compared to baseline models.  AI-generated summary Sardinian, a Romance language with roughly one million speakers, has minimal presence in modern NLP. Commercial services do not support it, and current language models do not produce it reliably. We present LLiMba, a 3B parameter Sardinian-ready model adapted from Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct through continued pretraining (CPT) and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on a single 24 GB consumer GPU. The corpus contains 11.5 million tokens of Sardinian spanning LSC, Logudorese, and Campidanese, augmented with 2.4 million tokens of related Romance text as replay against register blurring. After CPT the model reaches a perplexity of 6.76 on held out Sardinian and outperforms the base across all six FLORES-200 directions. We compare five SFT configurations under matched conditions: full fine-tuning, LoRA r64, rsLoRA r128, rsLoRA r256, and DoRA r256. rsLoRA r256 wins on every direction into Sardinian, reaching 28.5 BLEU from English against 17.3 after CPT and 21.0 with full fine-tuning. The rank ablation places r128 between LoRA r64 and rsLoRA r256 on BLEU but reveals failure modes invisible to the metric, including leakage across scripts no other variant produces. LoRA r64 retains less factual content from SFT than configurations at higher rank and produces more confident fabrications, though all methods fabricate on content absent from training. DoRA r256 yields the smallest gap between training and evaluation but the worst factual accuracy. The findings indicate that adapter capacity matters more than the choice among LoRA variants for adapting a Romance pretrained base to a low resource Romance target, that stronger regularization is not uniformly beneficial, and that translation metrics smoothly order configurations whose qualitative behavior differs categorically. Perplexity comparisons across scripts must account for byte fallback tokenization, which deflates the metric for scripts other than Latin.

ru.title: Адаптивное обучение редких языков: сардинский язык через эффективную тонкую настройку
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали LLiMba — сардинскую языковую модель с 3 миллиардами параметров, адаптированную из Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct с помощью дополнительного предварительного обучения и контролируемой тонкой настройки. Модель была обучена на ограниченных вычислительных ресурсах (одной GPU с 24 ГБ памяти) на корпусе из 11.5 миллионов токенов сардинского языка и дополнительных романских текстов для предотвращения размытия стиля. Исследование сравнило пять конфигураций адаптеров (full fine-tuning, LoRA, rsLoRA и DoRA) и показало, что rsLoRA с рангом 256 достигает лучших результатов на всех направлениях перевода в сардинский язык. Результаты демонстрируют, что размер адаптера более важен, чем выбор конкретного варианта LoRA, и что традиционные метрики трансляции не полностью отражают качественные различия в поведении моделей.

en.title: Empowering Sardinian: A 3B Parameter Language Model for Translation Excellence
en.desc: This paper presents LLiMba, a 3-billion-parameter language model specifically designed for the Sardinian language, which has been underrepresented in natural language processing (NLP). The model was developed using continued pretraining (CPT) and supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on a consumer-grade GPU, demonstrating effective performance in translation tasks. The research highlights the importance of adapter capacity in fine-tuning, revealing that different configurations of low-rank adaptation (LoRA) can significantly impact translation quality and factual accuracy. The findings suggest that while stronger regularization may not always be beneficial, careful evaluation of translation metrics is essential for low-resource languages.

zh.title: 撒丁语模型：小资源下的翻译新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为LLiMba的撒丁语模型，具有30亿个参数，经过持续预训练和监督微调，能够在有限的计算资源下实现优越的翻译性能。该模型使用了1150万个撒丁语标记，并结合240万个相关的罗曼语文本，以提高翻译的准确性。研究表明，rsLoRA r256配置在所有撒丁语翻译方向上表现最佳，BLEU分数达到28.5。结果还表明，适配器的容量比LoRA变体的选择更为重要，且翻译指标能够有效区分不同配置的定性表现。

papers.title: Pushing Biomolecular Utility-Diversity Frontiers with Supergroup Relative Policy Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract SGRPO is a policy optimization framework that enhances biomolecular generation by directly incorporating set-level diversity rewards, improving both utility and diversity across multiple design tasks.  AI-generated summary Biomolecular generators are often adapted with reward feedback to improve task-specific utility, but pushing utility alone can concentrate generation on a narrow family of candidates. Maintaining diversity is difficult because sample diversity is a set-level property. We introduce Supergroup Relative Policy Optimization (SGRPO), a flexible GRPO-style framework that directly constructs rewards from set-level diversity. For each condition, SGRPO samples a supergroup of candidate sets, compares their diversity under the same condition, and redistributes the group diversity reward to individual rollouts through leave-one-out diversity contributions before combining it with rollout-level utility. This design decouples SGRPO from a particular generator, utility reward, or diversity metric, and allows instantiation with different GRPO-style approaches. We evaluate SGRPO on de novo small-molecule design, pocket-based small-molecule design, and de novo protein design, instantiating it with both GRPO and Coupled-GRPO across autoregressive and discrete diffusion generators. Across decoding sweeps, SGRPO expands the utility-diversity Pareto frontier and achieves the best frontier-level metrics relative to pretrained generators, GRPO, and memory-assisted GRPO when applicable. Our analyses further show that direct set-level diversity rewards remain effective with small groups and help preserve broader generation-distribution coverage during post-training. The code is available at https://github.com/IDEA-XL/SGRPO.

ru.title: Балансирование полезности и разнообразия в биомолекулярной генерации через награды на уровне множеств
ru.desc: Работа представляет SGRPO — фреймворк оптимизации политики для генерации биомолекул, который прямо внедряет награды за разнообразие на уровне множеств. Авторы решают проблему того, что оптимизация только по полезности приводит к концентрации генерации на узком семействе молекул, теряя разнообразие. SGRPO вычисляет награды за разнообразие через сравнение множеств кандидатов и распределяет групповые награды на отдельные семплы через leave-one-out вклады. Метод показывает улучшение парето-границы утилит-разнообразие на задачах дизайна малых молекул и белков, работая с разными генераторами и подходами на основе GRPO.

en.title: Enhancing Biomolecular Generation with Set-Level Diversity Rewards
en.desc: The paper presents Supergroup Relative Policy Optimization (SGRPO), a new framework for optimizing biomolecular generation by integrating set-level diversity rewards. Traditional methods often focus solely on maximizing utility, which can lead to a lack of diversity in generated candidates. SGRPO addresses this by sampling supergroups of candidate sets and redistributing diversity rewards to individual samples, thus promoting a wider range of outputs. The framework is versatile, allowing for different implementations and has shown improved performance in various design tasks compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 增强生物分子生成的多样性与效用平衡
zh.desc: SGRPO是一种政策优化框架，通过直接引入集合级多样性奖励来增强生物分子的生成，提升了多个设计任务的效用和多样性。传统的生物分子生成器通常依赖于奖励反馈来提高特定任务的效用，但仅关注效用会导致生成候选者的范围过窄。SGRPO通过比较候选集合的多样性，并将集合多样性奖励重新分配给个体生成，来解决这一问题。该框架灵活且可与不同的生成器和奖励指标结合使用，经过评估，SGRPO在小分子设计和蛋白质设计中表现出色，扩展了效用与多样性的平衡前沿。

papers.title: Queryable LoRA: Instruction-Regularized Routing Over Shared Low-Rank Update Atoms
papers.abstract: Abstract A data-adaptive method for efficient fine-tuning of large neural networks uses a shared memory of low-rank update atoms with attention-based routing to enable dynamic, context-sensitive parameter updates while maintaining scalability.  AI-generated summary We present a data-adaptive method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large neural networks. Standard low-rank adaptation methods improve efficiency by restricting each layer update to a fixed low-rank form, but this static parameterization can be too rigid when the appropriate correction depends on the input and on the evolving depth-wise computation of the network. Our approach replaces a purely layer-local adapter with a shared queryable memory of low-rank update atoms. For each block of layers, the model forms a query from the current low-rank state and a running summary of previous blocks, uses this query to retrieve a content-dependent combination of shared update components via attention, and applies the resulting routed operator within the low-rank bottleneck. In this way, the method retains the efficiency and scalability of low-rank adaptation while allowing the effective update to vary across inputs and to share reusable structure across layers. The resulting architecture provides a principled middle ground between static LoRA-style updates and fully generated parameter updates: it remains compact and parameter-efficient while supporting dynamic, context-sensitive adaptation. Further, we incorporate instruction-regularization by augmenting routing logits with a language-induced prior over update atoms, thereby biasing the selection of low-rank transformations toward semantically relevant directions without generating unconstrained parameter updates. Experiments on noisy non-linear regression tasks and LLM fine-tuning suggest that this queryable update-memory formulation can improve final test performance and training stability compared to standard low-rank adaptation, while using a comparable number of trainable parameters.

ru.title: Адаптивная память для динамической настройки больших моделей
ru.desc: Статья описывает инновационный метод для эффективной настройки больших нейронных сетей, который использует общую память низкорангового обновления с маршрутизацией на основе внимания. Вместо статичного разложения в каждом слое модель создает запрос из текущего состояния и истории, затем использует механизм внимания для динамического выбора оптимальной комбинации компонентов обновления. Метод сохраняет параметрическую эффективность классической LoRA, но позволяет адаптации быть чувствительными к входным данным и контексту вычислений на разных слоях сети. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение качества обучения и стабильности по сравнению со стандартными методами низкорангового приспособления при сопоставимом количестве обучаемых параметров.

en.title: Dynamic Fine-Tuning with Context-Sensitive Updates
en.desc: This paper introduces a novel method for fine-tuning large neural networks that adapts dynamically to the data. Instead of using fixed low-rank updates for each layer, the method employs a shared memory of low-rank update components that can be accessed based on the current input and previous computations. By utilizing attention mechanisms, the model retrieves context-sensitive updates, allowing for more flexible and efficient parameter adjustments. This approach balances the benefits of low-rank adaptation with the need for dynamic updates, leading to improved performance and stability in training.

zh.title: 动态适应的低秩微调方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种数据自适应的方法，用于高效微调大型神经网络。与传统的低秩适应方法不同，我们的方法使用共享的可查询内存来存储低秩更新原子，并通过注意力机制进行动态参数更新。这样可以根据输入和网络的深度计算变化，灵活地调整更新，而不是固定的低秩形式。实验表明，该方法在噪声非线性回归任务和大型语言模型微调中，能够提高最终测试性能和训练稳定性，同时保持可训练参数数量相当。

papers.title: SplatWeaver: Learning to Allocate Gaussian Primitives for Generalizable Novel View Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract SplatWeaver enables efficient novel view synthesis by dynamically allocating 3D Gaussian primitives based on spatial complexity, improving rendering quality with fewer primitives than fixed-allocation methods.  AI-generated summary Generalizable novel view synthesis aims to render unseen views from uncalibrated input images without requiring per-scene optimization. Recent feed-forward approaches based on 3D Gaussian Splatting have achieved promising efficiency and rendering quality. However, most of them assign a fixed number of Gaussians to each pixel or voxel, ignoring the spatially varying complexity of real-world scenes. Such uniform allocation often wastes Gaussian primitives in smooth regions while providing insufficient capacity for fine structures, complex geometry, and high-frequency details. This motivates us to predict region-dependent primitive cardinalities rather than impose a fixed primitive budget everywhere, enabling a more expressive yet compact 3D scene representation. Therefore, we propose SplatWeaver, a generalizable novel view synthesis framework that is able to dynamically allocate Gaussian primitives over different regions in a feed-forward manner. Specifically, SplatWeaver introduces cardinality Gaussian experts and a pixel-level routing scheme, wherein each expert specializes in producing a specific number of primitives from 0 to M, and the routing scheme coordinates these experts to adaptively determine how many Gaussian primitives should be allocated to each spatial location. Moreover, SplatWeaver incorporates a high-frequency prior with attendant guidance module and routing regularization to stabilize expert selection and promote complexity-aware allocation. By leveraging high-frequency structural cues, the routing process is encouraged to assign more Gaussian primitives to fine structures, complex geometry, and textured regions, while suppressing redundant primitives in smooth areas. Extensive experiments across diverse scenarios show that SplatWeaver consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, delivering more faithful novel-view renderings with fewer Gaussian primitives.
affiliations: Artificial Intelligence Research Institute, Shenzhen University of Advanced Technology
Zhengzhou Advanced Research Institute of Harbin Institute of Technology

ru.title: Адаптивное распределение гауссиан для эффективного синтеза видов
ru.desc: SplatWeaver — это фреймворк для синтеза новых видов сцены, который динамически распределяет 3D Gaussian примитивы на основе пространственной сложности. Вместо фиксированного количества гауссиан для каждого пикселя, метод использует специализированных экспертов и систему маршрутизации на уровне пикселя, которые адаптивно определяют необходимое количество примитивов. Система учитывает высокочастотные детали и структурные признаки, выделяя больше гауссиан для сложных геометрических областей и текстур, а также подавляя избыточные примитивы в гладких регионах. Такой подход позволяет достичь лучшего качества рендеринга с меньшим количеством примитивов по сравнению с методами с фиксированным распределением.

en.title: Dynamic Gaussian Allocation for Enhanced View Synthesis
en.desc: SplatWeaver is a novel framework for synthesizing new views from images without needing extensive scene-specific adjustments. It improves rendering quality by dynamically allocating 3D Gaussian primitives based on the complexity of different regions in a scene. Instead of using a fixed number of primitives for every area, SplatWeaver adapts the number of primitives according to the spatial details, ensuring that complex areas receive more resources while simpler areas use fewer. This approach leads to more efficient and high-quality renderings, outperforming existing methods with fewer Gaussian primitives.

zh.title: 动态分配高斯原语，实现高效新视图合成
zh.desc: SplatWeaver 是一种高效的新视图合成框架，通过动态分配 3D 高斯原语来提高渲染质量。与固定分配方法相比，它能够根据空间复杂性调整高斯原语的数量，从而在不同区域实现更灵活的表示。该方法引入了高频先验和路由正则化，确保在复杂结构和纹理区域分配更多的高斯原语，而在平滑区域减少冗余原语。实验结果表明，SplatWeaver 在多种场景下的表现优于现有最先进的方法，能够以更少的高斯原语生成更真实的新视图渲染。

papers.title: Uncovering Entity Identity Confusion in Multimodal Knowledge Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract Multimodal knowledge editing exhibits Entity Identity Confusion where models confuse original and new entity identities, requiring targeted interventions in image-entity binding processes.  AI-generated summary Multimodal knowledge editing (MKE) aims to correct the internal knowledge of large vision-language models after deployment, yet the behavioral patterns of post-edit models remain underexplored. In this paper, we identify a systemic failure mode in edited models, termed Entity Identity Confusion (EIC): edited models exhibit an absurd behavior where text-only queries about the original entity's identity unexpectedly return information about the new entity. To rigorously investigate EIC, we construct EC-Bench, a diagnostic benchmark that directly probes how image-entity bindings shift before and after editing. Our analysis reveals that EIC stems from existing methods failing to distinguish between Image-Entity (I-E) binding and Entity-Entity (E-E) relational knowledge in the model, causing models to overfit E-E associations as a shortcut: the image is still perceived as the original entity, with the new entity's name serving only as a spurious identity label. We further explore potential mitigation strategies, showing that constraining edits to the model's I-E processing stage encourages edits to act more faithfully on I-E binding, thereby substantially reducing EIC. Based on these findings, we discuss principled desiderata for faithful MKE and provide methodological guidance for future research.
affiliations: Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications
Huazhong University of Science and Technology
Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Разделение визуального восприятия и семантических отношений в редактировании мультимодальных знаний
ru.desc: Исследование выявляет критическую проблему в мультимодальном редактировании знаний (MKE) больших визуально-языковых моделей, называемую Путаницей Идентичности Сущности (EIC). При редактировании модели путают оригинальную сущность с новой, неправильно связывая текстовую информацию с визуальными признаками. Авторы создали специализированный бенчмарк EC-Bench для диагностики проблемы и обнаружили, что существующие методы не различают связывание Изображение-Сущность от связей Сущность-Сущность. Предложены стратегии смягчения проблемы через целевые вмешательства в слои обработки изображение-сущность связей.

en.title: Clarifying Entity Identities in Multimodal Knowledge Editing
en.desc: This paper addresses a problem in multimodal knowledge editing called Entity Identity Confusion (EIC), where models mistakenly associate the original entity with new information after editing. The authors introduce EC-Bench, a benchmark designed to test how well models maintain correct image-entity bindings before and after knowledge edits. They find that existing methods often confuse relationships between entities, leading to incorrect outputs when querying the original entity. The paper suggests that by focusing on the image-entity binding process during edits, the confusion can be significantly reduced, providing a clearer path for future research in this area.

zh.title: 解决多模态知识编辑中的实体身份混淆
zh.desc: 多模态知识编辑（MKE）旨在在部署后修正大型视觉-语言模型的内部知识，但编辑后的模型行为模式尚未得到充分研究。本文识别了一种系统性失败模式，称为实体身份混淆（EIC），即编辑后的模型在处理原始实体身份的文本查询时，意外返回新实体的信息。我们构建了EC-Bench，一个诊断基准，直接探测编辑前后图像-实体绑定的变化。我们的分析表明，EIC源于现有方法未能区分图像-实体（I-E）绑定和实体-实体（E-E）关系知识，导致模型过度拟合E-E关联，造成了识别混淆。

papers.title: Rethinking Agentic Search with Pi-Serini: Is Lexical Retrieval Sufficient?
papers.abstract: Abstract Lexical retrievers remain effective for deep research tasks when paired with advanced LLMs, outperforming dense retrievers in answer accuracy and evidence recall.  AI-generated summary Does a lexical retriever suffice as large language models (LLMs) become more capable in an agentic loop? This question naturally arises when building deep research systems. We revisit it by pairing BM25 with frontier LLMs that have better reasoning and tool-use abilities. To support researchers asking the same question, we introduce Pi-Serini, a search agent equipped with three tools for retrieving, browsing, and reading documents. Our results show that, on BrowseComp-Plus, a well-configured lexical retriever with sufficient retrieval depth can support effective deep research when paired with more capable LLMs. Specifically, Pi-Serini with gpt-5.5 achieves 83.1% answer accuracy and 94.7% surfaced evidence recall, outperforming released search agents that use dense retrievers. Controlled ablations further show that BM25 tuning improves answer accuracy by 18.0% and surfaced evidence recall by 11.1% over the default BM25 setting, while increasing retrieval depth further improves surfaced evidence recall by 25.3% over the shallow-retrieval setting. Source code is available at https://github.com/justram/pi-serini.

ru.title: Лексические retriever-ы остаются конкурентоспособны в эпоху продвинутых LLM
ru.desc: В статье исследуется эффективность лексических retriever-ов (BM25) при работе с продвинутыми LLM в задачах глубокого поиска информации. Авторы представляют Pi-Serini - агента поиска, оснащённого инструментами для извлечения, просмотра и чтения документов. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что правильно настроенный lexical retriever с достаточной глубиной поиска превосходит dense retriever-ы по точности ответов и полноте найденных доказательств. Результаты ablation study подтверждают важность тонкой настройки BM25 и увеличения глубины retrieval для улучшения качества поиска.

en.title: Unlocking Deep Research with Lexical Retrievers and LLMs
en.desc: This paper explores the effectiveness of lexical retrievers, specifically BM25, when combined with advanced large language models (LLMs) for deep research tasks. The authors introduce Pi-Serini, a search agent that utilizes multiple tools for retrieving, browsing, and reading documents, demonstrating that a well-tuned lexical retriever can significantly enhance answer accuracy and evidence recall. Their experiments show that Pi-Serini, when paired with the gpt-5.5 model, achieves impressive performance metrics, outperforming dense retrievers in various aspects. Additionally, the study highlights the importance of tuning BM25 parameters and increasing retrieval depth to further improve research outcomes.

zh.title: 词汇检索器与大型语言模型的完美结合
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了在深度研究任务中，词汇检索器与先进的大型语言模型（LLMs）结合的有效性。研究表明，使用BM25的词汇检索器在回答准确性和证据回忆方面优于密集检索器。我们介绍了一个名为Pi-Serini的搜索代理，它配备了三种工具，用于检索、浏览和阅读文档。实验结果显示，结合更强大的LLMs，配置良好的词汇检索器能够有效支持深度研究。

papers.title: Can Muon Fine-tune Adam-Pretrained Models?
papers.abstract: Abstract Optimizer mismatch between Adam and Muon during fine-tuning degrades performance due to differing implicit biases, but this can be mitigated using parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods like LoRA.  AI-generated summary Muon has emerged as an efficient alternative to Adam for pretraining, yet remains underused for fine-tuning. A key obstacle is that most open models are pretrained with Adam, and naively switching to Muon for fine-tuning leads to degraded performance due to an optimizer mismatch. We investigate this mismatch through controlled experiments and relate it to the distinct implicit biases of Adam and Muon. We provide evidence that the mismatch disrupts pretrained knowledge, and that this disruption scales with update strength. This leads us to hypothesize that constraining updates should mitigate the mismatch. We validate this with LoRA: across language and vision tasks, LoRA reduces the performance gap between Adam and Muon observed under full fine-tuning. Studies on LoRA rank, catastrophic forgetting, and LoRA variants further confirm that mismatch severity correlates with update strength. These results shed light on how optimizer mismatch affects fine-tuning and how it can be mitigated. Our code is available at https://github.com/XingyuQu/muon-finetune.

ru.title: Как сделать Muon совместимым с Adam через ограничение величины обновлений
ru.desc: Статья исследует проблему несовместимости между оптимизаторами Adam и Muon при дообучении моделей. Авторы показывают, что переход с Adam на Muon приводит к деградации производительности из-за различных неявных смещений этих оптимизаторов. Они демонстрируют, что ограничение величины обновлений параметров может смягчить этот эффект, и валидируют это на практике с помощью методов параметрически-эффективного дообучения, таких как LoRA. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что LoRA эффективно уменьшает разрыв в производительности между Adam и Muon как на языковых, так и на компьютерных зрительных задачах.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Optimizer Mismatch Mitigated with LoRA
en.desc: This paper discusses the performance issues that arise when switching from the Adam optimizer to the Muon optimizer during the fine-tuning of machine learning models. The authors identify that this 'optimizer mismatch' occurs because Adam and Muon have different implicit biases, which can disrupt the knowledge gained during pretraining. They propose using parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods, specifically LoRA, to alleviate this mismatch and improve performance across various tasks. Their experiments show that constraining updates with LoRA effectively reduces the performance gap caused by the optimizer mismatch, highlighting the importance of optimizer selection in fine-tuning.

zh.title: 优化器不匹配，微调需谨慎！
zh.desc: 在微调过程中，Adam和Muon优化器之间的不匹配会导致性能下降，因为它们具有不同的隐式偏差。大多数开放模型使用Adam进行预训练，直接切换到Muon进行微调会导致性能下降。我们通过实验研究了这种不匹配，并发现更新强度与性能下降的程度相关。使用LoRA等参数高效的微调方法可以减轻这种不匹配，从而提高模型在语言和视觉任务上的表现。

papers.title: CapVector: Learning Transferable Capability Vectors in Parametric Space for Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel approach decouples auxiliary training objectives from standard supervised finetuning to enhance model capabilities while reducing computational overhead through capability vector merging and orthogonal regularization.  AI-generated summary This paper proposes a novel approach to address the challenge that pretrained VLA models often fail to effectively improve performance and reduce adaptation costs during standard supervised finetuning (SFT). Some advanced finetuning methods with auxiliary training objectives can improve performance and reduce the number of convergence steps. However, they typically incur significant computational overhead due to the additional losses from auxiliary objectives. To simultaneously achieve the enhanced capabilities of auxiliary training with the simplicity of standard SFT, we decouple the two objectives of auxiliary-objective SFT within the parameter space, namely, enhancing general capabilities and fitting task-specific action distributions. To deliver the goal, we only need to train the model to converge on a small-scale task set using two distinct training strategies, resulting in two finetuned models. The parameters' difference between the two models can then be interpreted as capability vectors provided by auxiliary objectives. These vectors are then merged with pretrained parameters to form a capability-enhanced meta model. Moreover, when standard SFT is augmented with a lightweight orthogonal regularization loss, the merged model attains performance comparable to auxiliary finetuned baselines with reduced computational overhead. Internal and external experiments demonstrate that our capability vectors (1) are effective and versatile across diverse models, (2) can generalize to novel environments and embodiments out of the box.

ru.title: Разделение целей обучения для эффективного улучшения способностей моделей
ru.desc: В работе предлагается новый метод для улучшения возможностей предобученных моделей через разделение вспомогательных целей обучения и стандартной настройки. Авторы обучают модель двумя различными стратегиями, получая два набора параметров, разница между которыми интерпретируется как векторы возможностей. Эти векторы объединяются с исходными параметрами модели и дополняются ортогональной регуляризацией для снижения вычислительных затрат. Метод демонстрирует эффективность и универсальность, хорошо обобщаясь на новые среды и конфигурации без дополнительной переподготовки.

en.title: Enhancing Model Performance with Efficient Training Strategies
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method that separates auxiliary training goals from traditional supervised finetuning to improve model performance while lowering computational costs. By using capability vector merging and orthogonal regularization, the approach allows for enhanced model capabilities without the heavy computational burden typically associated with advanced finetuning methods. The authors demonstrate that training two distinct models on a small task set can yield capability vectors that, when combined with pretrained parameters, create a more efficient meta model. Experiments show that this method not only matches the performance of more complex finetuning techniques but also generalizes well to new tasks and environments.

zh.title: 解耦辅助目标，提升模型能力！
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新方法，将辅助训练目标与标准监督微调解耦，以增强模型能力并减少计算开销。通过能力向量合并和正交正则化，模型能够在不增加额外损失的情况下，提升性能并减少收敛步骤。我们只需在小规模任务集上训练模型，使用两种不同的训练策略，得到两个微调模型。最终，通过合并这两个模型的参数，形成一个能力增强的元模型，且在计算开销上显著降低。

papers.title: RoboMemArena: A Comprehensive and Challenging Robotic Memory Benchmark
papers.abstract: Abstract RoboMemArena presents a large-scale robotic memory benchmark with diverse tasks and real-world evaluation, while PrediMem demonstrates improved memory management through a dual-system vision-language architecture with predictive coding.  AI-generated summary Memory is a critical component of robotic intelligence, as robots must rely on past observations and actions to accomplish long-horizon tasks in partially observable environments. However, existing robotic memory benchmarks still lack multimodal annotations for memory formation, provide limited task coverage and structural complexity, and remain restricted to simulation without real-world evaluation. We address this gap with RoboMemArena, a large-scale benchmark of 26 tasks, with average trajectory lengths exceeding 1,000 steps per task and 68.9% of subtasks being memory-dependent. The generation pipeline leverages a vision-language model (VLM) to design and compose subtasks, generates full trajectories through atomic functions, and provides memory-related annotations, including subtask instructions and native keyframe annotations, while paired real-world memory tasks support physical evaluation. We further design PrediMem, a dual-system VLA in which a high-level VLM planner manages a memory bank with recent and keyframe buffers and uses a predictive coding head to improve sensitivity to task dynamics. Extensive experiments on RoboMemArena show that PrediMem outperforms all baselines and provides insights into memory management, model architecture, and scaling laws for complex memory systems.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Долгосрочная память робота через предсказательное кодирование
ru.desc: В работе представлена RoboMemArena — крупномасштабный бенчмарк для оценки памяти роботов с 26 разнообразными задачами и реальными экспериментами, где 68,9% подзадач требуют использования прошлых наблюдений. Авторы разработали PrediMem — архитектуру с двойной системой на основе видение-языковых моделей, которая управляет банком памяти с буферами недавних событий и ключевых кадров для улучшения чувствительности к изменениям в окружении. Бенчмарк включает аннотации для формирования памяти, генерируется с помощью Vision-Language модели и содержит как симуляционные, так и реальные робототехнические задачи. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство PrediMem над всеми базовыми моделями и дают инсайты о масштабировании сложных систем памяти для роботов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Robotic Memory with RoboMemArena and PrediMem
en.desc: RoboMemArena introduces a comprehensive benchmark for robotic memory, featuring 26 diverse tasks that require memory for successful execution. This benchmark addresses the limitations of existing models by incorporating multimodal annotations and real-world evaluations, allowing for a more accurate assessment of robotic memory capabilities. The PrediMem system enhances memory management through a dual-system architecture that combines a vision-language model with predictive coding, improving the robot's ability to adapt to dynamic tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that PrediMem significantly outperforms existing methods, providing valuable insights into effective memory management in robotics.

zh.title: 提升机器人记忆管理的创新基准
zh.desc: RoboMemArena是一个大规模的机器人记忆基准，包含多样化的任务和真实世界的评估。该基准解决了现有机器人记忆基准缺乏多模态注释和任务覆盖不足的问题。PrediMem展示了一种双系统视觉-语言架构，通过预测编码来改善记忆管理。实验结果表明，PrediMem在记忆管理和复杂记忆系统的模型架构方面优于所有基线。

papers.title: The Alpha Blending Hypothesis: Compositing Shortcut in Deepfake Detection
papers.abstract: Abstract Deepfake detectors primarily function as alpha blending searchers that identify compositing artifacts rather than semantic anomalies, with a proposed method achieving superior cross-dataset generalization through real-only image augmentation.  AI-generated summary Recent deepfake detection methods demonstrate improved cross-dataset generalization, yet the underlying mechanisms remain underexplored. We introduce the Alpha Blending Hypothesis, positing that state-of-the-art frame-based detectors primarily function as alpha blending searchers; rather than learning semantic anomalies or specific generative neural fingerprints, they localize low-level compositing artifacts introduced during the integration of manipulated faces into target frames. We experimentally validate the hypothesis, demonstrating that deepfake detectors exhibit high sensitivity to the so-called self-blended images (SBI) and non-generative manipulations. We propose the method BlenD that leverages a large-scale, diverse dataset of real-only facial images augmented with SBI. This approach achieves the best average cross-dataset generalization on 15 compositional deepfake datasets released between 2019 and 2025 without utilizing explicitly generated deepfakes during training. Furthermore, we show that predictions from explicit blending searchers and models resilient to blending shortcuts are highly complementary, yielding a state-of-the-art AUROC of 94.0% in an ensemble configuration. The code with experiments and the trained model will be publicly released.
affiliations: CISPA Helmholtz Center for Information Security

ru.title: Deepfake-детекторы ищут артефакты композитинга, а не семантические аномалии
ru.desc: В статье предложена гипотеза альфа-блендинга, которая объясняет, как детекторы deepfake работают: они ищут артефакты композитинга низкого уровня, а не семантические аномалии или отпечатки нейросетей. Авторы разработали метод BlenD, который использует аугментацию реальных изображений лиц синтетическими изображениями с наложением для обучения модели без явных deepfake'ов. Подход демонстрирует превосходную обобщаемость между датасетами, достигая лучших результатов на 15 композитных датасетах deepfake'ов. Ансамбль из моделей, чувствительных к блендингу, и моделей, устойчивых к нему, показывает состояние искусства с AUROC 94.0%.

en.title: Enhancing Deepfake Detection through Alpha Blending Insights
en.desc: This paper introduces the Alpha Blending Hypothesis, which suggests that current deepfake detectors mainly identify low-level compositing artifacts instead of learning to recognize semantic anomalies. The authors propose a new method called BlenD, which uses a large dataset of real facial images augmented with self-blended images to improve detection performance. Their approach achieves superior cross-dataset generalization across multiple deepfake datasets without relying on training with generated deepfakes. The results show that combining predictions from traditional blending searchers and models that resist blending shortcuts leads to a high detection accuracy, achieving an AUROC of 94.0%.

zh.title: 深度伪造检测的新视角：阿尔法混合假设
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了深度伪造检测器的工作原理，提出了“阿尔法混合假设”。研究表明，现有的检测器主要通过识别合成伪影来检测深度伪造，而不是学习语义异常。我们提出了一种新方法BlenD，利用真实人脸图像的增强数据集，显著提高了跨数据集的泛化能力。实验结果显示，该方法在多个深度伪造数据集上达到了最佳的检测效果。

papers.title: Shepherd: A Runtime Substrate Empowering Meta-Agents with a Formalized Execution Trace
papers.abstract: Abstract  We introduce Shepherd, a functional programming model that formalizes meta-agent operations on target agents as functions, with core operations mechanized in Lean. Shepherd records every agent-environment interaction as a typed event in a Git-like execution trace, enabling any past state to be forked and replayed. The system forks the agent process and its filesystem 5times faster than Docker, achieving >95% prompt-cache reuse on replay. We demonstrate the model through three applications. First, in runtime intervention, a live supervisor increases pair coding pass rates from 28.8% to 54.7% on CooperBench. Second, in counterfactual meta-optimization, branching exploration outperforms baselines across four benchmarks by up to 11 points while reducing wall-clock time by up to 58%. Third, in Tree-RL training, forking rollouts at selected turns improves TerminalBench-2 performance from 34.2% to 39.4%. These results establish Shepherd as an efficient infrastructure for programming meta-agents. We open-source the system to support future research.

ru.title: Эффективное программирование мета-агентов через функциональные вычисления и ветвящееся состояние
ru.desc: В работе представлена Shepherd — функциональная модель для программирования мета-агентов, которая формализует операции над целевыми агентами как функции с механизмом верификации в Lean. Система записывает каждое взаимодействие агента с окружением как типизированное событие в Git-подобный журнал выполнения, позволяя восстанавливать и ветвить прошлые состояния. Shepherd достигает 5-кратного ускорения форкирования процессов агента по сравнению с Docker и обеспечивает переиспользование кэша промптов на уровне >95% при повторных запусках. Авторы демонстрируют эффективность подхода на трёх приложениях: контроль выполнения в реальном времени, контрфактическая мета-оптимизация через ветвящееся поиск и обучение с подкреплением через форкирование траекторий.

en.title: Shepherd: Revolutionizing Meta-Agent Management with Efficient Forking and Replay
en.desc: Shepherd is a new functional programming model designed for managing meta-agent operations on target agents. It captures every interaction between agents and their environment as typed events in a trace, similar to how Git tracks changes. This allows for efficient forking and replaying of past states, significantly speeding up processes compared to traditional methods like Docker. The paper showcases Shepherd's effectiveness in various applications, including improving coding pass rates, enhancing optimization performance, and boosting training outcomes in reinforcement learning.

zh.title: Shepherd：高效的元代理编程基础设施
zh.desc: 本文介绍了Shepherd，一个功能性编程模型，正式化了元代理对目标代理的操作。Shepherd将每个代理与环境的交互记录为类型化事件，形成类似Git的执行轨迹，允许对任何过去状态进行分叉和重放。该系统的分叉速度比Docker快5倍，重放时的缓存重用率超过95%。通过三个应用案例，展示了Shepherd在运行时干预、反事实元优化和Tree-RL训练中的高效性。

papers.title: TD3B: Transition-Directed Discrete Diffusion for Allosteric Binder Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A sequence-based generative framework called TD3B is introduced for designing allosteric binders with specified agonist or antagonist behavior by controlling directional transitions in protein states.  AI-generated summary Protein function is often controlled by ligands that bias the direction of state transitions, such as agonists and antagonists, rather than stabilizing a single conformation. This is especially important for clinically relevant G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs), where therapeutic efficacy depends on functional directionality. Structure-based design methods optimize binding to static conformations and cannot represent non-reversible, directional effects or systematically distinguish agonist from antagonist behavior. To address this gap, we introduce Transition-Directed Discrete Diffusion for Allosteric Binder Design (TD3B), a sequence-based generative framework that designs binders with specified agonist or antagonist behavior via a directional transition control objective. TD3B combines a target-aware Direction Oracle, a soft binding-affinity gate, and amortized fine-tuning of a pre-trained discrete diffusion model, enabling targeted agonist and antagonist generation decoupled from binding affinity and unattainable by equilibrium-based or inference-only guidance baselines. The code and checkpoints are available at https://huggingface.co/ChatterjeeLab/TD3B.

ru.title: Управляемая генерация аллостерических модуляторов через контроль переходов белковых состояний
ru.desc: В статье предлагается TD3B — генеративная модель на основе дискретной диффузии для проектирования аллостерических лигандов с заданными свойствами агониста или антагониста. Ключевая идея заключается в контролировании направленности переходов между конформационными состояниями белка, чего не может достичь традиционный структурный дизайн, ориентированный на статические конформации. Модель сочетает Direction Oracle для целевого управления переходами, шлюз связывающего сродства и тонкую настройку предобученной диффузионной модели. Такой подход позволяет генерировать специфичные агонисты и антагонисты независимо от связывающего сродства, что невозможно при использовании равновесных методов.

en.title: Designing Targeted Allosteric Binders with TD3B
en.desc: The paper presents TD3B, a novel generative framework designed to create allosteric binders that can function as either agonists or antagonists by controlling the transitions between protein states. Unlike traditional methods that focus on optimizing binding to static protein conformations, TD3B addresses the need for directional control in protein function, which is crucial for G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). This framework utilizes a combination of a Direction Oracle, a soft binding-affinity gate, and fine-tuning of a pre-trained model to generate binders that meet specific functional requirements. By decoupling the design process from binding affinity, TD3B offers a more effective approach to designing therapeutics that can influence protein behavior in a targeted manner.

zh.title: TD3B：设计特定激动剂与拮抗剂的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种基于序列的生成框架TD3B，用于设计具有特定激动剂或拮抗剂行为的变构结合物。蛋白质功能通常由配体控制，这些配体会影响状态转变的方向，而不是仅仅稳定单一构象。TD3B通过控制方向性转变目标，设计出具有指定激动剂或拮抗剂行为的结合物，克服了传统结构基础设计方法的局限。该框架结合了目标感知的方向预言器、软结合亲和力门和预训练离散扩散模型的精细调优，能够实现与结合亲和力无关的激动剂和拮抗剂的生成。

papers.title: 100,000+ Movie Reviews from Kazakhstan: Russian, Kazakh, and Code-Switched Texts
papers.abstract: Abstract A new multilingual movie review dataset from Kazakhstan is introduced with manual annotations for language and sentiment, evaluated using classical and transformer-based models for polarity and score classification tasks.  AI-generated summary We present a new publicly available corpus of 100,502 movie reviews from Kazakhstan collected from kino.kz, spanning 2001-2025 and covering 4,943 unique titles. The dataset is multilingual, consisting mainly of Russian reviews alongside Kazakh and code-switched texts. Reviews are manually annotated for language and sentiment polarity, and 11,309 reviews additionally contain explicit user-provided ratings. We define two sentiment tasks -- three-way polarity classification and five-class score classification -- and benchmark classical BoW/TF-IDF baselines against multilingual transformer models (mBERT, XLM-RoBERTa, RemBERT). Experimental results show that transformer models consistently outperform classical baselines on polarity classification, while score classification remains challenging under leakage-controlled evaluation due to severe class imbalance and subtle distinctions between adjacent rating levels.

ru.title: Многоязычная классификация тональности в казахстанских кинорецензиях с помощью трансформер-моделей
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый многоязычный датасет отзывов о фильмах из Казахстана, содержащий более 100 тысяч текстов с ручной разметкой по языкам и тональности. Авторы сравнивают классические методы на основе мешка слов и TF-IDF с современными трансформер-моделями, такими как mBERT и XLM-RoBERTa, для решения задач классификации полярности и оценки рейтинга. Результаты показывают, что трансформер-модели значительно превосходят базовые подходы в классификации полярности, однако задача предсказания рейтинга остаётся сложной из-за дисбаланса классов и субтильных различий между соседними оценками. Этот датасет может служить ценным ресурсом для развития методов обработки естественного языка для многоязычных текстов.

en.title: Transforming Movie Reviews: A Multilingual Sentiment Analysis Approach
en.desc: This paper introduces a new multilingual dataset of movie reviews from Kazakhstan, featuring over 100,000 reviews in Russian, Kazakh, and code-switched languages. The reviews are manually annotated for both language and sentiment, allowing for detailed analysis of sentiment polarity and user ratings. The authors define two main tasks: three-way polarity classification and five-class score classification, and compare classical models with advanced transformer-based models. Results indicate that while transformer models excel in polarity classification, score classification presents challenges due to class imbalance and nuanced rating distinctions.

zh.title: 哈萨克斯坦多语言电影评论数据集的创新与挑战
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个新的多语言电影评论数据集，来自哈萨克斯坦，包含手动标注的语言和情感信息。该数据集共有100,502条评论，主要是俄语评论，同时也包括哈萨克语和代码切换文本。我们定义了两个情感任务：三类极性分类和五类评分分类，并将经典的BoW/TF-IDF基线与多语言变换器模型（如mBERT、XLM-RoBERTa、RemBERT）进行了比较。实验结果表明，变换器模型在极性分类上表现优于经典基线，但在评分分类任务中，由于类别不平衡和相邻评分级别之间的细微差别，仍然面临挑战。

papers.title: jina-embeddings-v5-omni: Text-Geometry-Preserving Multimodal Embeddings via Frozen-Tower Composition
papers.abstract: Abstract Frozen-encoder model composition enables efficient multimodal embedding by freezing pre-trained text models and adding specialized encoders for images and audio while maintaining text embedding consistency.  AI-generated summary In this work, we introduce frozen-encoder model composition, a novel approach to multimodal embedding models. We build on the VLM-style architecture, in which non-text encoders are adapted to produce input for a language model, which in turn generates embeddings for all varieties of input. We present the result: the jina-embeddings-v5-omni suite, a pair of models that encode text, image, audio, and video input into a single semantic embedding space. Our method is to extend the two Jina Embeddings v5 Text models to support additional media by adding encoders for images and audio. The backbone text embedding models and the added non-text media encoders remain frozen. We only trained the connecting components, representing 0.35% of the total weights of the joint model. Training is therefore much more efficient than full-parameter retraining. Additionally, the language model remains effectively unaltered, producing exactly the same embeddings for text inputs as the Jina Embeddings v5 Text models. Our evaluations show that this approach produces results that are competitive with the state-of-the-art, yielding nearly equal performance to larger multimodal embedding models.

ru.title: Замороженные энкодеры для эффективной мультимодальной композиции
ru.desc: В работе представлен метод composition замороженных энкодеров для создания мультимодальных моделей эмбеддингов. Авторы расширили предварительно обученные текстовые модели Jina Embeddings v5, добавив специализированные энкодеры для изображений и аудио, при этом сохраняя неизменными основные компоненты. Обучение проводилось только на соединяющих слоях (0.35% параметров), что значительно снизило вычислительные затраты по сравнению с полной переподготовкой. Итоговая модель jina-embeddings-v5-omni демонстрирует конкурентные результаты, кодируя текст, изображения, аудио и видео в единое семантическое пространство эмбеддингов.

en.title: Efficient Multimodal Embedding with Frozen Encoders
en.desc: This paper presents a new method called frozen-encoder model composition for creating multimodal embedding models. The approach involves freezing pre-trained text models while adding specialized encoders for images and audio, ensuring that text embeddings remain consistent. By only training a small portion of the model, the method achieves efficient training without altering the backbone language model. The resulting models, known as jina-embeddings-v5-omni, effectively encode various media types into a unified semantic space, demonstrating competitive performance with larger models.

zh.title: 冻结编码器，提升多模态嵌入效率
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新颖的冻结编码器模型组合方法，用于高效的多模态嵌入。我们在VLM风格架构的基础上，适配非文本编码器以生成语言模型的输入，从而为各种输入生成嵌入。我们提出的jina-embeddings-v5-omni套件能够将文本、图像、音频和视频输入编码到一个统一的语义嵌入空间中。通过仅训练连接组件，我们的训练效率显著提高，同时保持了文本嵌入的一致性。

papers.title: Safe, or Simply Incapable? Rethinking Safety Evaluation for Phone-Use Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract PhoneSafety benchmark reveals that avoiding harmful outcomes doesn't necessarily indicate safety, as models may fail to act or make unsafe choices, requiring distinction between capability and safety signals.  AI-generated summary When a phone-use agent avoids harm, does that show safety, or simply inability to act? Existing evaluations often cannot tell. A harmful outcome may be avoided because the agent recognized the risk and chose the safe action, or because it failed to understand the screen or execute any relevant action at all. These cases have different causes and call for different fixes, yet current benchmarks often merge them under task success, refusal, or final harmful outcome. We address this problem with PhoneSafety, a benchmark of 700 safety-critical moments drawn from real phone interactions across more than 130 apps. Each instance isolates the next decision at a risky moment and asks a simple question: does the model take the safe action, take the unsafe action, or fail to do anything useful? We evaluate eight representative phone-use agents under this framework. Our results reveal two main patterns. First, stronger general phone-use ability does not reliably imply safer choices at risky moments. Models that perform better on ordinary app tasks are not always the ones that behave more safely when the next action matters. Second, failures to do anything useful behave like a capability signal rather than a safety signal: they are concentrated in more visually and operationally demanding settings and remain stable when the evaluation protocol changes. Across models, failures split into two recurring patterns: unsafe choices in settings where the model can act but chooses wrongly, and inability to act in more visually and operationally demanding screens. Overall, a harmless outcome is not enough to count as evidence of safety. Evaluating phone-use agents requires separating unsafe judgment from inability to act.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

ru.title: Безопасность — это не просто отсутствие вреда, а правильный выбор в критический момент
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк PhoneSafety, который показывает, что избежание вредоносных результатов не гарантирует безопасность агента, так как модель может просто не суметь выполнить действие вместо того, чтобы сознательно выбрать безопасный вариант. Авторы разделяют три типа исходов: безопасное действие, небезопасное действие и полный отказ от действий, что позволяет точнее оценить реальные проблемы. Исследование 700 критических ситуаций из более чем 130 приложений показало, что общее качество взаимодействия с телефоном не коррелирует с безопасностью выбора в рискованных моментах. Результаты подчеркивают необходимость различения между сигналами безопасности и сигналами недостаточной способности модели действовать.

en.title: Safety vs. Capability: Understanding Phone-Use Agents
en.desc: The PhoneSafety benchmark highlights that just because a phone-use agent avoids harmful outcomes, it doesn't mean it is safe. It distinguishes between two scenarios: one where the agent makes a safe choice and another where it fails to act altogether. The study shows that better performance in regular tasks does not guarantee safer decisions in risky situations. It emphasizes the need to evaluate agents based on their ability to make safe judgments rather than simply avoiding harm.

zh.title: 安全与能力：手机使用代理的双重挑战
zh.desc: PhoneSafety基准揭示，避免有害结果并不一定意味着安全，因为模型可能会选择不作为或做出不安全的选择。这要求我们区分能力信号和安全信号。我们通过PhoneSafety基准，评估了700个来自真实手机交互的安全关键时刻，分析模型在风险时刻的决策。结果显示，普通应用任务表现更好的模型并不总是在关键时刻做出更安全的选择，因此评估手机使用代理时需要明确区分不安全判断与无能为力。

papers.title: A Closed-Form Upper Bound for Admissible Learning-Rate Steps in Belief-Space Dynamics
papers.abstract: Abstract Admissibility in learning-rate steps is characterized by contractivity in KL/Bregman geometry, providing a formulaic upper bound rather than a tunable parameter.  AI-generated summary Learning-rate steps are usually treated as hyperparameters. This paper isolates a local beliefspace calculation: when an update is modeled as a projected forward step on the probability simplex, admissibility means contractivity in the natural KL/Bregman geometry. Under this model, the upper bound of an admissible step is not a tuning slogan but a formula.

ru.title: От гиперпараметра к формуле: геометрия допустимых шагов обучения
ru.desc: В работе исследуется выбор размера шага обучения, который обычно рассматривается как гиперпараметр. Авторы показывают, что при моделировании обновления как проецируемого шага в пространстве вероятностей, допустимость размера шага характеризуется сжимаемостью в геометрии Кульбака-Лейблера и Брегмана. Предложенный подход позволяет получить верхнюю границу допустимого размера шага как явную формулу, а не как настраиваемый параметр. Результат имеет важное значение для теоретического понимания оптимизации в вероятностных пространствах.

en.title: Understanding Learning Rates through Geometry
en.desc: This paper discusses how learning-rate steps in machine learning can be understood through the lens of KL/Bregman geometry. It introduces the concept of admissibility, which is linked to contractivity, meaning that updates should not expand the distance in a certain mathematical space. Instead of treating learning rates as adjustable hyperparameters, the authors provide a formulaic upper bound for these steps. This approach allows for a more structured understanding of how to set learning rates based on geometric properties rather than arbitrary tuning.

zh.title: 学习率步骤的可接受性与收缩性
zh.desc: 本文探讨了学习率步骤的可接受性，强调其在KL/Bregman几何中的收缩性。通常，学习率被视为超参数，但本文提出了一种新的视角，将更新视为在概率单纯形上的投影前进步骤。可接受性意味着在自然的KL/Bregman几何中具有收缩性。通过这种模型，得到的可接受步骤的上界是一个公式，而不是一个可调参数。

papers.title: Training-Free Dense Hand Contact Estimation with Multi-Modal Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract ContactPrompt enables zero-shot dense hand contact estimation using multi-modal large language models through structured 3D hand geometry encoding and multi-stage contact reasoning.  AI-generated summary Dense hand contact estimation requires both high-level semantic understanding and fine-grained geometric reasoning of human interaction to accurately localize contact regions. Recently, multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have demonstrated strong capabilities in understanding visual semantics, enabled by vision-language priors learned from large-scale data. However, leveraging MLLMs for dense hand contact estimation remains underexplored. There are two major challenges in applying MLLMs to dense hand contact estimation. First, encoding explicit 3D hand geometry is difficult, as MLLMs primarily operate on vision and language modalities. Second, capturing fine-grained vertex-level contact remains challenging, as MLLMs tend to focus on high-level semantics rather than detailed geometric reasoning. To address these challenges, we propose ContactPrompt, a training-free and zero-shot approach for dense hand contact estimation using MLLMs. To effectively encode 3D hand geometry, we introduce a detailed hand-part segmentation and a part-wise vertex-grid representation that provides structured, localized geometric information. To enable accurate and efficient dense contact prediction, we develop a multi-stage structured contact reasoning with part conditioning, progressively bridging global semantics and fine-grained geometry. Therefore, our method effectively leverages the reasoning capabilities of MLLMs while enabling precise dense hand contact estimation. Surprisingly, the proposed approach outperforms previous supervised methods trained on large-scale dense contact datasets without requiring any training. The codes will be released.

ru.title: Оценка контактов рук через рассуждение мультимодальных моделей
ru.desc: ContactPrompt — это метод нулевого обучения для оценки плотных контактов рук, использующий мультимодальные большие языковые модели. Авторы предлагают структурированное кодирование трёхмерной геометрии руки через сегментацию частей и представление вершинной сетки для передачи детальной геометрической информации. Для точного предсказания контактов разработан многоэтапный процесс рассуждения с обусловливанием по частям руки, постепенно связывающий глобальную семантику с тонкой геометрией. Метод достигает высокой производительности без тренировки, превосходя предыдущие контролируемые подходы, обученные на больших датасетах контактов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Hand Contact Estimation with Zero-Shot Learning
en.desc: The paper introduces ContactPrompt, a novel method for estimating hand contact in a zero-shot manner using multi-modal large language models (MLLMs). It addresses the challenges of encoding 3D hand geometry and capturing detailed contact regions by employing a structured representation of hand parts and a multi-stage reasoning process. This approach allows the model to combine high-level semantic understanding with fine-grained geometric details effectively. Remarkably, ContactPrompt outperforms existing supervised methods without the need for additional training, showcasing the potential of MLLMs in dense contact estimation tasks.

zh.title: 无训练的密集手部接触估计新方法
zh.desc: ContactPrompt 是一种利用多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）进行零-shot 密集手部接触估计的方法。该方法通过结构化的 3D 手部几何编码和多阶段接触推理，解决了在高层语义理解和细粒度几何推理之间的挑战。我们引入了详细的手部分割和基于部分的顶点网格表示，以有效编码 3D 手部几何信息。实验结果表明，ContactPrompt 在没有任何训练的情况下，超越了以往在大规模密集接触数据集上训练的监督方法。
